seoTitle: How to Connect and Sync Two Airtable Bases
metaDescription: Share live data between separate Airtable bases using Airtable Sync: generate a share token in the source base and connect it in the destination.
intro: When two teams or projects live in entirely separate Airtable bases but need to share the same underlying data, Airtable Sync creates a live, token-based connection instead of requiring manual copy-pasting. This guide covers generating the share link in your source base and connecting it as a synced table in the destination.

steps.heading: Open your source base view
steps.body: Go to the specific grid view in your source base that contains the data you want to share.

steps.heading: Enable Share and Sync
steps.body: Click the 'Share and sync' view component to generate a secure cross-base data sync token.

steps.heading: Allow syncing to other bases
steps.body: Toggle the option that permits this view to be synced into other bases.

steps.heading: Copy the share token
steps.body: Copy the unique cross-base share link and token to your clipboard.

steps.heading: Create a synced table in the destination base
steps.body: Switch to your destination base, add a new table, and select the 'Airtable Base' option under the sync data feed directory.

steps.heading: Paste the token and map fields
steps.body: Paste your token, map the corresponding columns, and save to initiate the live sync, reviewing the automatically synchronized grid afterward.

faq.question: Is Airtable Sync a two-way connection?
faq.answer: No, Airtable Sync is one-way — the destination base receives updates from the source, but changes in the destination don't flow back.

faq.question: How often does synced data update?
faq.answer: Sync intervals can be configured, and you can also set field visibility permissions on what data is shared through the connection.

faq.question: Do I need edit access to both bases to set up a sync?
faq.answer: You need sharing permissions on the source view and the ability to create tables in the destination base.

faq.question: Can I edit synced records in the destination base?
faq.answer: Synced tables are generally read-only reflections of the source data, since edits would be overwritten on the next sync.
